title: Confirm web research capability and source capture schema

web_access.method: Fetched pages with Python urllib from this worker environment; captured HTTP status, final URL, content type, title/meta evidence.

checks.title: Intro to Claude - Claude API Docs
checks.evidence: Fetched HTML contained title/meta description for Claude API Docs.

checks.title: OpenAI Platform
checks.evidence: Fetched official OpenAI Platform page; requested docs URL redirected to platform landing page.

checks.title: Continue - open-source AI code agent - Visual Studio Marketplace
checks.evidence: Fetched marketplace HTML; meta description identified it as a Visual Studio Code extension and open-source AI code agent.

checks.title: GitHub - continuedev/continue: ⏩ Source-controlled AI checks, enforceable in CI. Powered by the open-source Continue CLI · GitHub
checks.evidence: Fetched repository page HTML and title metadata.

suggested_recovery_if_web_fails_later: Record the failing URL, command/method, HTTP/network error, and date in the worker handoff.
Retry with a primary-source alternate URL such as official docs, marketplace, GitHub, or package registry.
If network remains blocked, stop substantive product-status claims and request source excerpts from the orchestrator/user.

top_level_fields.last_checked: ISO date YYYY-MM-DD for the most recent source check in this handoff.
top_level_fields.sources: Array of source objects using source_fields.
top_level_fields.claims: Array of claim objects using claim_fields; every nontrivial factual claim should cite source_ids or be marked inference/unsupported.
top_level_fields.options: Array of option objects using option_fields when evaluating products, plugins, APIs, or architectures.
top_level_fields.blockers: Array of blocker objects with blocker, impact, and suggested_recovery.
top_level_fields.confidence: Overall confidence enum value for the handoff.

source_fields.source_id: Stable local id such as src-openai-pricing-api.
source_fields.title: Page or artifact title.
source_fields.final_url: Final URL after redirects, if checked.
source_fields.publisher: Organization or project publishing the source.
source_fields.source_type: official_docs | pricing_page | marketplace_listing | source_repository | package_registry | release_notes | issue_or_discussion | vendor_blog | third_party_analysis | direct_test | other
source_fields.http_status: HTTP status when web-fetched, or null if not fetched directly.
source_fields.quoted_evidence: Short quote or metadata snippet supporting cited claims.

claim_fields.claim_id: Stable local id such as claim-t010-openai-api-billing.
claim_fields.text: Atomic factual claim, inference, risk, or recommendation.
claim_fields.claim_type: subscription_constraint | api_access | billing | ux_mode | integration | project_status | privacy_or_policy_risk | performance_or_latency | recommendation | other
claim_fields.source_ids: Array of source_id values; empty only when evidence_type is inference or unsupported.

option_fields.option_id: Stable id for a product, plugin, extension, API path, or architecture.
option_fields.name: Human-readable option name.
option_fields.category: vscode_extension | obsidian_plugin | editor_or_fork | api_provider | local_model_stack | proxy_or_gateway | custom_architecture | other
option_fields.source_ids: Array of source_id values supporting option metadata.
option_fields.claim_ids: Array of claim_id values supporting evaluation.

access_model_fields.subscription_ui_access: supported | not_supported | unknown | not_applicable; whether an existing ChatGPT/Claude-style UI subscription can be used directly in this option.
access_model_fields.official_api_access: supported | not_supported | unknown | not_applicable; whether the option uses an official API path/key.
access_model_fields.requires_separate_api_billing: yes | no | unknown | not_applicable; whether API usage is billed separately from the UI subscription.
access_model_fields.unofficial_session_automation: none_required | optional | required | reported | unknown | not_applicable; whether cookies/browser/session scraping or reverse-engineered access is involved.
access_model_fields.subscription_names: Array naming relevant subscriptions, e.g. ChatGPT Plus, Claude Pro, Claude Max, Team, Enterprise.
access_model_fields.risk_level: none | low | medium | high | unknown; especially for unofficial automation, ToS, brittleness, account, or privacy risks.
access_model_fields.access_notes: Short caveat text with source_ids or claim_ids where possible.

confidence_guidance.high: Current primary source directly supports the claim and no material conflict was found.
confidence_guidance.medium: Source support is credible but indirect, partial, single-source, or likely to drift.
confidence_guidance.low: Weak, stale, secondary, conflicting, or mostly inferential support.

mode_taxonomy_definitions.true_inline_tab: Copilot-like ghost text appears inline while typing and can be accepted with Tab or a direct equivalent in the editor.
mode_taxonomy_definitions.inline_not_tab: Inline/ghost text exists but Tab acceptance is absent, disabled, or not evidenced.
mode_taxonomy_definitions.completion_popup: Suggestions appear primarily in an editor completion menu/popup rather than ghost text.
mode_taxonomy_definitions.command_or_selection_generation: User invokes a command/prompt/action to generate or transform selected text; not continuous live autocomplete.
mode_taxonomy_definitions.chat_or_sidebar: Assistant works mainly through chat/sidebar/panel UI.
mode_taxonomy_definitions.custom_only: Feasible only by building custom glue, extension, plugin, or proxy; no ready packaged UX found.
mode_taxonomy_definitions.not_applicable: The item is not an editor UX surface or autocomplete mode does not apply.

handoff_instructions_for_T010_T040_T050: Cite primary sources for pricing, API/subscription boundaries, marketplace capabilities, and project status.
Classify every evaluated option with exactly one autocomplete_mode from the taxonomy.
Populate access_model for every option so synthesis can distinguish UI subscription reuse, official API use, separate API billing, and unofficial session automation.
Mark unsupported or speculative claims explicitly with evidence_type=inference_from_sources or unsupported and confidence=low/unknown.
